title: Reward gain and punishment avoidance reversal learning
description: This dataset comprises two reversal learning tasks designed to investigate differential neural processing of reward gain and punishment avoidance. Participants performed parallel tasks using distinct reinforcers: monetary reward (gain/non-gain feedback) and primary threat avoidance (noise burst avoidance/application), with noise intensity individually titrated to match subjective value of monetary reward across participants.
methods_description: Two reversal learning tasks were administered with different reinforcement contingencies. The reward task provided positive feedback indicating monetary gain (+10 cents) or non-reward (+0 cents), while the punishment task used positive feedback to signal successful avoidance of a loud white noise burst or negative feedback indicating noise application. White noise intensity was individually calibrated (81-90 dB) for each participant to equate subjective value with monetary reward.
